channel.title: +Yew Lee kuan on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Yew Lee kuan on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Japanophilia
item.description: I saw an unforgettable example of how they took pride in their work during a visit in the late 1970s to Takamatsu, a city on the island of Shikoku. The Japanese ambassador gave me dinner at their best but three-star hotel. The food was excellent. For fruit and dessert, a chef in his 30s appeared in immaculate white to demonstrate his skill with a sharp knife, peeling persimmons and crunchy pears.

item.title: singapore-days
item.description: … in eradicating corruption. It is said that his brother, a police officer, was corrupt and forced him to resign. During his rule, Singapore created a Housing Board which developed huge colonies of flats built for the poorer and middle classes. It was ensured that the colonies were multi-racial in composition. These colonies are enviable models for third world and developing countries. The Singapore Port and Harbour are equally enviable models for even the most developed countries, with its highly advanced computerisation, containerisation and unbelievably rapid turnround of ships.

item.title: spiegel 2005
